memo: System architecture review of the Template-free AI Scientist. Identified three key issues: (1) ambiguity in system boundaries due to reliance on manual filtering despite claims of no human modification; (2) missing independent reliability evaluation for the internal LLM evaluators guiding tree search; (3) risk of discarding valuable terminal feedback by not integrating the Automated Reviewer's outputs into subsequent search steps. Recommendations target boundary clarification, evaluator bias evaluation, and feedback loop integration.

diagnostics.statement: There is an ambiguity regarding the extent of the system's autonomy, as the text asserts the workflow requires no human modification but also reports manual human filtering of the most promising outputs at each stage.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The overall process was then run to generate ideas, experiments and papers. We manually filtered the most promising outputs at each stage (Supplementary Information section A.4). Had this filtering not occurred, the papers under analysis would still have been produced in their final form, just along with other papers and, thus, at a greater total cost. This process resulted in three complete manuscripts being selected for submission.
The entire scientific workflow for each paper, from ideation and coding to manuscript writing, was performed without any human modification.

diagnostics.consequence: The reliance on manual filtering obscures the agentic-tree search's true success rate and makes it impossible to assess the system's independent reliability in consistently producing high-quality research without human intervention.

diagnostics.statement: The system utilizes an LLM-based evaluator and GPT-4o to assess leaf nodes and select paths for expansion, but lacks an independent reliability study for these specific internal evaluation mechanisms.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: At the end of each stage, an LLM-based evaluator assesses all leaf nodes and selects the most promising one to serve as the root for the next stage of exploration, thus effectively pruning less promising research avenues.

When choosing between non-buggy nodes, the system uses a best-first search strategy guided by GPT-4o, which evaluates candidates based on factors like performance metrics, training dynamics and the quality of the plots generated. The selected nodes are expanded by creating a new child node.

diagnostics.consequence: Without evaluating the evaluator for internal node selection, the tree search could be systematically biased, improperly pruning viable research paths or hallucinating positive evaluations for flawed paths.

diagnostics.statement: The Automated Reviewer provides numerical scores and a preliminary decision on the final manuscript, but there is a risk that this terminal evaluation is not fed back to improve the system's prior steps.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The agent processes the PDF of a manuscript to produce a structured review, including numerical scores for soundness, presentation and contribution, along with a list of strengths and weaknesses and a preliminary accept or reject decision (Supplementary Information section A.3).

diagnostics.consequence: Failing to integrate terminal feedback limits the system's ability to recursively self-correct, meaning the system may repeatedly generate manuscripts with similar structural or scientific flaws.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The overall process was then run to generate ideas, experiments and papers. We manually filtered the most promising outputs at each stage (Supplementary Information section A.4). Had this filtering not occurred, the papers under analysis would still have been produced in their final form, just along with other papers and, thus, at a greater total cost. This process resulted in three complete manuscripts being selected for submission.
The entire scientific workflow for each paper, from ideation and coding to manuscript writing, was performed without any human modification.

success_check.observable: Documentation of the exact criteria, frequency, and volume of human filtering at each stage.
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: System boundary explicitly defines human-in-the-loop filtering steps rather than claiming zero human modification.
success_check.evidence_needed: A revised workflow diagram or textual description clearly labeling the human filtering stages and reporting the ratio of discarded to selected outputs.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: At the end of each stage, an LLM-based evaluator assesses all leaf nodes and selects the most promising one to serve as the root for the next stage of exploration, thus effectively pruning less promising research avenues.

When choosing between non-buggy nodes, the system uses a best-first search strategy guided by GPT-4o, which evaluates candidates based on factors like performance metrics, training dynamics and the quality of the plots generated. The selected nodes are expanded by creating a new child node.

success_check.observable: An independent empirical evaluation of the LLM-based evaluator and GPT-4o best-first search strategies.
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Agreement rate with independent human experts on node pruning/selection is quantified.
success_check.evidence_needed: A property-specific reliability study comparing the internal LLM evaluations of leaf nodes and best-first search selections against a ground-truth dataset labeled by human scientists.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The agent processes the PDF of a manuscript to produce a structured review, including numerical scores for soundness, presentation and contribution, along with a list of strengths and weaknesses and a preliminary accept or reject decision (Supplementary Information section A.3).

success_check.observable: Implementation of a feedback loop using the Automated Reviewer's structured output.
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Automated Reviewer weaknesses logically map to generated revisions or new tree-search constraints.
success_check.evidence_needed: Source code or system trace demonstrating that the strengths, weaknesses, and scores identified by the Automated Reviewer are fed back as prompts into the ideation, experimentation, or revision modules.
